svg.text: N-Queens as a Benchmark & Real-World Connection
• Constraint-solver benchmark: every CSP and SAT solver paper benchmarks N-Queens.
• GPU/parallel benchmarks: demonstrates work-stealing, branch divergence, Amdahl effects.
• Distributed-computing teaching example: shard by first row's column choice.
• n=27 took ~1 month on a 24-machine cluster (TU Dresden 2016) — a notable HPC milestone.
• Jeff Somers' bitmask C code is the canonical fastest serial implementation.
• Underlies more practical CSP problems: time-table, frequency-assignment, sensor placement.
• N-Rooks: only column conflicts → trivial, n! solutions.
• N-Queens completion problem: given a partial board, can it be completed? NP-complete.
• Toroidal N-Queens: diagonals wrap around — solutions exist iff gcd(n, 6) = 1.
• N-Queens dominating set: minimum queens to attack every cell — NP-hard.
• 1D variant (any-step queens, super-queens): different attack rules, different counts.
• N-Queens with extra blockers: subset of cells forbidden — combinatorial completion problem.
N-Queens completion is NP-complete; the placement problem itself is not (heuristic O(n) constructive).
